root_text.sn47.37:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.37:1.4: Tassa kāye kāyānupassino viharato yo kāyasmiṁ chando so pahīyati.
root_text.sn47.37:2.1: Vedanāsu vedanānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.37:2.2: Tassa vedanāsu vedanānupassino viharato yo vedanāsu chando so pahīyati.
root_text.sn47.37:3.1: Citte cittānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.37:3.2: Tassa citte cittānupassino viharato yo cittamhi chando so pahīyati.
root_text.sn47.37:4.1: Dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.37:4.2: Tassa dhammesu dhammānupassino viharato yo dhammesu chando so pahīyati.

translation_text.sn47.37:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn47.37:1.3: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.37:1.4: As they do so they give up desire for the body.
translation_text.sn47.37:1.5: When desire is given up they realize freedom from death.
translation_text.sn47.37:2.1: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.37:2.2: As they do so they give up desire for feelings.
translation_text.sn47.37:2.3: When desire is given up they realize freedom from death.
translation_text.sn47.37:3.1: They meditate observing an aspect of the mind—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.37:3.2: As they do so they give up desire for the mind.
translation_text.sn47.37:3.3: When desire is given up they realize freedom from death.
translation_text.sn47.37:4.1: They meditate observing an aspect of principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.37:4.2: As they do so they give up desire for principles.
translation_text.sn47.37:4.3: When desire is given up they realize freedom from death.”
